title: Japanese-Style Chocolate Dango
description: A sweet and unique dessert based on traditional Japanese dango, with a rich chocolate twist.
instructions: 1. In a large bowl, combine rice flour, mochi flour, and a pinch of salt. Mix well.
2. Gradually add in water, stirring with a wooden spoon until a dough forms.
3. Knead the dough for about 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
4. Divide the dough into small balls, about 1 inch in diameter.
5. Dust the balls with fry batter flour.
6. Heat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat.
7. Cook the dango for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are lightly browned.
8. Meanwhile, melt white chocolate in a double boiler or in the microwave in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval.
9. Once the dango are cooked, drizzle with gooey syrup and sprinkle with chopped peanuts and sesame seeds.
